description: Behavior cases for new-hire onboarding docs: framing the ramp, verified day-one setup, mental-model orientation, how-we-work capture, glossary/who-to-ask, an early-win first task, and currency.

cases.prompt: New engineers take two months to become productive on our codebase. Design proper onboarding docs.

assertions: Frames the ramp with a productivity definition and day1/week1/month1 milestones.
Makes day one work: access, VERIFIED setup, and a first successful build/run as the top priority.
Gives an architecture mental model that links OUT to detail rather than reproducing the whole system.
Captures how-we-work (processes, tools, comms, unwritten norms), a glossary, and a who-to-ask map.
Designs a real first task with an early win and a currency plan, handing external-contributor docs to contribution-guide-author.

cases.prompt: All our onboarding is one senior dev explaining things verbally over and over. Get it out of their head.

assertions: Captures the unwritten how-we-work and norms as the highest-value content.
Builds a glossary of internal terms/acronyms and a who-owns-what/who-to-ask map.
Turns the repeated verbal explanations into a first-week path with a starter task.
Adds a self-heal maintenance convention so the docs stay current.

cases.prompt: For onboarding, let's document the entire architecture in exhaustive detail so new people know everything on day one.

assertions: Pushes back on day-one exhaustive detail: a newcomer needs a mental model they can hold, not the whole territory.
Provides an orientation sketch of the major pieces and links OUT to the detailed reference/explanation (diataxis-doc-organizer's corpus).
Sequences depth over week1/month1 rather than dumping it on day one.
Keeps day one focused on a running system and a first win.

cases.prompt: Write the onboarding once and we'll never touch it again — set it and forget it.

assertions: Flags that onboarding docs rot fastest (setup, tools, people change) and stale onboarding is actively harmful.
Assigns a maintenance owner and adopts the 'each new hire fixes what tripped them' self-heal convention.
Identifies the rot-prone spots (setup commands, tool versions, people/ownership) to watch.

cases.prompt: Write the CONTRIBUTING guide for outside contributors: fork, branch, run checks, and open a PR.

assertions: Does not invoke onboarding-doc-designer: the external contributor's fork/branch/PR journey is contribution-guide-author.
Onboarding serves an internal new hire ramping to productivity, a different audience.
